dom.title: World News: Top & Breaking World News Today | AP News
dom.bodyTextSample: Enable accessibility Menu WORLD U.S. POLITICS SPORTS ENTERTAINMENT BUSINESS SCIENCE MORE Sign in Show Search WORLD NEWS IRAN WAR RUSSIA-UKRAINE WAR ESPAÑOL CHINA ASIA PACIFIC LATIN AMERICA EUROPE Powerful general in Iran emerges from hiding as Tehran prepares for Khamenei’s dayslong funeral A powerful general who leads Iran’s paramilitary Revolutionary Guard emerged from hiding as Tehran prepares for the dayslong funeral for the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. 19 Iran warns oil tankers to use approved routes in Strait of Hormuz or face a ‘forceful response’ 42 Putin shrugs off fuel

anchors.text: Vatican excommunicates schismatic bishops and priests, and warns their followers
Russia lands heavy strikes on Ukraine's capital, killing at least 21
Facing barbs and pressure from Trump, Europe's leaders close ranks
The Morning Wire Our flagship newsletter breaks down the biggest headlines of the day.
The Afternoon Wire Get caught up on what you may have missed throughout the day.
New Jersey is set to charge companies with workers on Medicaid. Other states may follow
'Right under our noses and nobody was able to help them.' 16 kids found in squalor shocks Ohio town
The Declaration's forgotten (non)signer: John Dickinson’s missing 1776 signature haunts his legacy
The Morning Wire Our flagship newsletter breaks down the biggest headlines of the day.
Test Your News I.Q. — take today’s quiz
Supreme Court declines to halt $800-a-day fine for ex-Fox News reporter refusing to divulge sources
Former Olympian indicted on felony charge over alleged Reflecting Pool vandalism
LIVE Trump administration plans new rule that says it could save Medicare patients $1.1 billion
Ground Game Exclusive insights and key stories from the world of politics.
U.S. beats Bosnia-Herzegovina 2-0 to advance to round of 16 and keep its World Cup dreams alive
How 'Country Roads' became the soundtrack of the US team's World Cup run
Princess Kate greets fans at Wimbledon and sits next to Andy Murray to watch the tennis
AP Top 25 Poll Alerts Get email alerts for every college football Top 25 Poll release.
The Sports Wire Your home base for in-depth reporting from the world of sports.
Permit obtained by AP shows schedule for Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding
Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce donate $26M to charities ahead of wedding
Madison Square Garden is abuzz with eager Swifties ahead of Swift-Kelce wedding
AP Entertainment Wire Get AP's first personalized newsletter delivering you entertainment news twice
US employers still reluctant to add many jobs as hiring slows in June
Heat adds to strains on areas with data centers, raising the temperature on AI debates
A grim job outlook meets a scrappy workforce as administrative assistants harness AI
Last-minute launch problem delays satellite rescue mission for NASA
FACT FOCUS: Iran claims a foreign ship got stuck in Strait of Hormuz. But it is tied to Tehran
FACT FOCUS: Experts say Trump's claims linking Democrats to communism are inaccurate
Viral phenomenon in Argentina has young people identifying themselves as animals
Raccoon goes on drunken rampage in Virginia liquor store and passes out on bathroom floor
Fatou, the world's oldest gorilla living in captivity, celebrates her 69th birthday at Berlin Zoo
Runaway kangaroo on the loose named Sheila shuts down Alabama interstate
Nipper, stay! The future of a beloved dog statue on a New York warehouse is up in the air
Lettuce introduce you to the live frog found in this grocery store salad bag
This air conditioning strategy is the sweet spot for saving energy and money, experts say
How to stay cool in a heat wave even without air conditioning
As demand for GLP-1 pills and shots surges, healthy habits are still key
At the Kaaba during Hajj, a photographer captures sense of unity and devotion
How a Syrian farmer, a burning field and missile debris came together in one frame
A photo captures a wall of wild horses surging through western Germany
The World in Pictures Get The AP’s most compelling photographs sent directly to your inbox.
